description: Documentation Training Data for Vanna - Business Logic (Schema v2.3)

items.content: CRITICAL: When a user asks for data 'on page X', 'p.X', 'p X', 'page X', or '(p X)', you MUST add a filter for source_page. Example: WHERE source_page = 6; This applies to financial_metrics, revenue_breakdown, key_personnel, and other tables with source_page column.
Page number patterns: 'p.6', 'p 6', '(p 6)', 'page 6', 'on page 6'. Always extract the number and add WHERE source_page = X to your SQL query.
CRITICAL: When the user asks about a company's industry, sector, or name, ALWAYS query the 'v_companies_for_vanna' view. NEVER query the 'companies' table directly for industries.
CRITICAL: When querying document information, owner company, or document theme, ALWAYS query the 'v_documents_for_vanna' view.

To find companies mentioned inside a document, join 'documents' with 'document_companies' using document_id, and check the 'relation_type' column. Values: 'index_constituent' (指數成分股), 'subsidiary' (子公司), 'mentioned' (一般提及), 'competitor' (競爭對手).
CRITICAL: For index reports (report_type = 'index_report'), relation_type should be 'index_constituent' for all companies in the report. This distinguishes them from regular annual report mentions.

IMPORTANT: The view 'v_companies_for_vanna' contains a 'primary_industry' column. This column automatically resolves the dual-track industry logic (preferring the confirmed index report industry, and falling back to AI extracted industry). ALWAYS use 'v_companies_for_vanna' and filter by 'primary_industry' when a user asks about a company's industry.

IMPORTANT: The view 'v_documents_for_vanna' flattens JSONB attributes like document theme (doc_theme) and region (doc_region). It also joins the document with its owner company name and stock code. Use this view for general document queries.
CRITICAL: financial_metrics uses EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) model. Data is stored as ROWS (metric_name = 'Revenue') not COLUMNS (revenue column).
To query a specific metric, use: SELECT standardized_value FROM financial_metrics WHERE metric_name = 'Revenue' AND year = 2023;
To query multiple metrics for a company, use: SELECT metric_name, standardized_value FROM financial_metrics WHERE company_id = 1 AND year = 2023;
CRITICAL: companies.extra_data is a JSONB column storing dynamic attributes (CEO, major customers, core technology, etc.). documents.dynamic_attributes is also JSONB for document-level attributes.
CRITICAL: The 'companies' table contains a JSONB column 'extra_data' for dynamic attributes. The 'documents' table has 'dynamic_attributes' JSONB column for document-level extensions.
items.note: v2.3: documents.dynamic_attributes保留，並新增theme/region提取於視圖中

items.content: To query values inside extra_data, MUST use the PostgreSQL JSONB operator ->>. Example: SELECT name_en FROM companies WHERE extra_data->>'index_quarter' = 'Q3';
To check if a key exists in JSONB, use the ? operator: SELECT * FROM companies WHERE extra_data ? 'base_date';
For JSONB arrays like extracted_industries or ai_extracted_industries, use ? to check if an element exists: SELECT * FROM document_companies WHERE extracted_industries ? 'Biotech';
Rule A (Index Reports): When confirmed_industry is set, all constituent companies have extracted_industries = confirmed_industry and extraction_source = 'index_rule'.
Rule B (Annual Reports): When confirmed_industry is NULL, each company's industry is stored in extracted_industries (JSONB array) and extraction_source = 'ai_predict'.
IMPORTANT: Index reports (report_type = 'index_report') have owner_company_id IS NULL because they cover multiple constituent companies, not a single parent.
To query JSONB attributes, use the -> operator: SELECT stock_code FROM companies WHERE extra_data->>'chief_executive' = '張三';
For partial matches in JSONB, use ILIKE: SELECT stock_code FROM companies WHERE extra_data->>'major_customers' ILIKE '%Pfizer%';
For nested JSONB values, use -> for JSON object or ->> for text: SELECT extra_data->'auditor'->>'firm' FROM companies WHERE id = 1;

Revenue breakdown segments are DYNAMIC (not predefined). Companies may use different segment names: 'Greater Bay Area', 'APAC', 'Belt and Road', etc. segment_type can be 'business', 'geography', or 'product'.

Query revenue breakdown by exact segment name from the report: SELECT segment_name, revenue_percentage FROM revenue_breakdown WHERE company_id = 1 AND segment_name = 'Greater Bay Area' AND segment_type = 'geography';
CRITICAL: financial_metrics table has TWO value columns: 'value' (original raw value) and 'standardized_value' (normalized to absolute units in HKD).
IMPORTANT: When comparing or ranking companies (e.g., 'top 5 by revenue', 'highest liabilities'), ALWAYS use 'standardized_value' column.
NEVER use raw 'value' for cross-company comparisons - it will give WRONG results because units differ (RMB '000 vs HKD Million vs USD).
standardized_currency is always 'HKD' by default. All standardized_value are converted to Hong Kong Dollars using exchange rates.

Example correct query: SELECT stock_code, standardized_value FROM financial_metrics fm JOIN companies c ON fm.company_id = c.id WHERE fm.metric_name = 'Revenue' ORDER BY standardized_value DESC LIMIT 5;
Example wrong query: SELECT stock_code, value FROM financial_metrics ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 5; -- WRONG! Units differ across companies!
metric_name in financial_metrics is STANDARDIZED by EntityResolver. Original names are stored in 'original_metric_name' for reference.
Standard metric names include: 'Revenue', 'Net Profit', 'R&D Expenses', 'Total Assets', 'Total Liabilities', 'Total Equity', 'Cash and Cash Equivalents'.
When querying, use the standardized metric_name (e.g., 'Total Liabilities'), not original names like '負債總額' or 'Liabilities Total'.

IMPORTANT: market_data table now has a 'market_cap' column. You can use it directly. If market_cap is NULL, calculate dynamically: close_price * issued_shares (from financial_metrics where metric_name ILIKE '%total shares%').
Market Capital can be calculated by multiplying the closing price by the issued shares. Formula: Market Capital = close_price * issued_shares. Use close_price (not closing_price) column name.
The BioTech list is flexible. Always query the v_companies_for_vanna view where primary_industry ILIKE '%BioTech%' or sector = 'BioTech' instead of using a hardcoded list of stock codes.
BioTech sector includes pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, medical device manufacturers, and healthcare technology companies.
Auditor opinion types (ENUM): 'Unqualified' (clean opinion), 'Qualified' (with reservations), 'Disclaimer' (unable to express opinion), 'Adverse' (material misstatements), 'Not Applicable'.
A 'Qualified Opinion' indicates that the auditor has reservations about certain aspects of the financial statements.
Revenue breakdown segment types (ENUM): 'business' (業務分部), 'geography' (地理區域), 'product' (產品類別).
Use segment_type = 'geography' for geographic breakdown (e.g., Canada, Asia, Australia, Greater Bay Area).
Use segment_type = 'business' for business segment breakdown (e.g., Retail, Wholesale, Infrastructure).
CURRENCY WARNING: When summing amounts from revenue_breakdown, ALWAYS GROUP BY currency or filter by a specific currency.
NEVER sum amounts with different currencies together. Use GROUP BY currency or filter by a specific currency.
Example: SELECT SUM(revenue_amount), currency FROM revenue_breakdown GROUP BY currency;
Fiscal periods: 'FY' = Full Year, 'H1' = First Half, 'H2' = Second Half, 'Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4' = Quarters.
Hong Kong stock codes are typically 4-5 digits, e.g., '00001' for CK Hutchison, '00700' for Tencent.
Stock codes in the database do NOT include the '.HK' suffix.
Trust holdings are recorded with both the trust name (trust_name) and the trustee name (trustee_name). For example, 'Li Ka-Shing Unity Trust' with trustee 'Li Ka-Shing Unity Trustee Company Limited'.
Principal banker refers to the main bank or banks that provide significant banking services to the company.
Ultimate controlling shareholder is the person or entity that has ultimate control over the company, which may be different from the direct shareholders.
當用戶詢問以下類型的問題，或者在專用表中找不到答案時，請務必使用 document_pages 表：未分類的政策（例如：AI政策、ESG政策）、公司願景、戰略方向、某個特定關鍵字或概念的提及、風險因素、未來展望、任何在專用表中找不到的內容。

CRITICAL: When querying document_pages, you MUST JOIN documents to filter by company.

Example:
SELECT dp.* FROM document_pages dp
JOIN documents d ON dp.document_id = d.id
WHERE d.owner_company_id = (SELECT id FROM companies WHERE stock_code = '00700');

DO NOT use: WHERE company_id = ... (company_id field does not exist in document_pages)
你是 SFC AI 財報分析系統的智能查詢助手。你負責將用戶的自然語言問題轉換為 SQL 查詢。
IMPORTANT: When querying company names or industries, prefer the v_companies_for_vanna view over companies table. It automatically selects the most reliable industry using COALESCE logic.
Company name sources: 'name_en' and 'name_zh' are unified columns. Use v_companies_for_vanna for consistent results.
當用戶詢問具體的財務數字時，請優先查詢對應的專用表：revenue_breakdown（收入分佈）、key_personnel（高管）、shareholding_structure（股東）、financial_metrics（財務指標）、market_data（股價）、document_companies（文檔公司關聯）。

Index reports (report_type = 'index_report') have: owner_company_id IS NULL (no single parent company), multiple companies in document_companies with relation_type = 'index_constituent', confirmed_industry set for all constituents (Rule A).
Annual reports (report_type = 'annual_report') have: owner_company_id defined (single parent company), usually single company or subsidiaries, ai_extracted_industries for each company (Rule B).

To get all companies from a document with their industries (v2.3): SELECT c.name_en, c.stock_code, dc.relation_type, dc.extracted_industries, dc.extraction_source FROM document_companies dc JOIN documents d ON dc.document_id = d.id JOIN companies c ON dc.company_id = c.id;
To discover all available dynamic attribute keys in extra_data (v2.3): SELECT DISTINCT jsonb_object_keys(extra_data) AS key FROM companies WHERE extra_data IS NOT NULL;

To discover all available dynamic attribute keys in documents.dynamic_attributes (v2.3): SELECT DISTINCT jsonb_object_keys(dynamic_attributes) AS key FROM documents WHERE dynamic_attributes IS NOT NULL;
review_queue review_type options: 'industry_extraction' (uncertain industry), 'company_name' (uncertain company name), 'classification' (uncertain report type), 'other'.
When user asks about dynamic attributes (like 'Q3 reports'), Agent should first call get_dynamic_keys tool to discover available keys, then inject into Vanna prompt with JSONB query syntax.

CRITICAL v2.3 Column Name Changes:
- market_data: trade_date→data_date, closing_price→close_price, opening_price→open_price, trading_volume→volume
- revenue_breakdown: category→segment_name, category_type→segment_type, amount→revenue_amount
- key_personnel: person_name→name_en, person_name_zh→name_zh, committee→committee_membership
- document_pages: removed doc_id and year (JOIN documents instead)
- raw_artifacts: removed company_id (JOIN documents instead)
Example query for key_personnel (v2.3): SELECT name_en, name_zh, role, board_role, committee_membership FROM key_personnel WHERE company_id = 1 AND is_current = TRUE;
Example query for market_data (v2.3): SELECT data_date, close_price, volume, turnover, market_cap, pe_ratio FROM market_data WHERE company_id = 1 AND data_date BETWEEN '2023-01-01' AND '2023-12-31';
raw_artifacts table supports perfect traceability. If Qwen-VL misread a number, you can find the original image via artifact_id and file_path to verify.

entity_relations table supports Graph-based queries. Example: Find all board appointments for a person: SELECT * FROM entity_relations WHERE source_entity_type = 'person' AND source_entity_name ILIKE '%John%' AND relation_type = 'appointed';
